summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a stellar ’69 show featuring a sublime Dark Star, soaring Morning Dew and the beloved Mountains of the Moon.
review: Reviewers rave about the musical heights reached on this unknown‑date 1969 concert – a melting Dark Star, an “astounding” Morning Dew, a hot The Other One and a beautifully mellow Mountains of the Moon.  The SBD source is praised as clear with only a few brief cuts, making it one of the best‑recorded versions of these classics.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (4–5) and enthusiastic comments about both performance and sound quality; minor recording glitches noted but do not detract from an outstanding show.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced SBD with occasional brief cuts (e.g., Lovelight) and a short dropout noted; overall very listenable and faithful to the band’s sound.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers highlight smooth, mellow transitions – Dupree's into Mountains of the Moon leading into a soaring Dark Star, followed by generous jams (Alligator > Drumz > Chant) and an extended Bid You Goodnight that caps the night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is repeatedly praised – “intro to Death Don't Hurt Me” described as his best, and overall sound noted as “especially Garcia”.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart’s drumming receives shout‑outs (Gyuto monks reference) and the Alligator > Drumz > Chant sequence is highlighted.
